lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: change-image-input-to-latent
summary: Explain socket contract changes, latent dict shape, function body changes, return type matching, and VAE boundary risks.
use_when: How do I change a ComfyUI custom node from IMAGE input to LATENT input?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: latent-dict-shape
summary: LATENT is a dict with samples tensor shaped [B,C,H,W]; IMAGE is [B,H,W,C].
use_when: What is the structure of a ComfyUI LATENT object?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: image-vs-latent-space
summary: Use IMAGE for pixel/PIL/OpenCV/RGB operations; use LATENT for denoising-space operations before VAE decode.
use_when: When should a node operate in IMAGE space versus LATENT space?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: multitype-inputs
summary: Prefer V3 MultiType or explicit optional image/latent inputs with validation; avoid unguarded wildcard sockets.
use_when: How can a ComfyUI node accept more than one input type?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: custom-datatypes
summary: Create custom uppercase datatypes for objects only your node pack understands; use forceInput for custom object sockets.
use_when: When should I create a custom ComfyUI datatype?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: return-type-mismatch
summary: Declared RETURN_TYPES must match returned tuple object types; single outputs need trailing comma.
use_when: Why does my custom node fail after I changed RETURN_TYPES?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: mask-shape-safety
summary: Normalize mask dimensionality; masks may be [B,H,W] and sometimes squeezed, so check shape before broadcasting.
use_when: How should custom nodes handle MASK shapes?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: vae-boundary
summary: Only if the algorithm truly needs pixel data or must cross image/latent spaces; make VAE boundary explicit.
use_when: Do I need VAE encode/decode when changing image nodes to latent nodes?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: socket-compatibility
summary: ComfyUI client enforces datatype compatibility to prevent wrong data form flowing through graph.
use_when: Why can’t I connect two ComfyUI nodes after changing types?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.

lane_title: ComfyUI Advanced Node I/O, Datatypes, and Socket Contracts
title: wildcard-risk
summary: Use only for advanced adapter/router nodes with validation; backend support is limited and the node must make sense of incoming data.
use_when: Should I use wildcard * inputs in ComfyUI?
chunking_hint: Retrieve canonical overview and official source anchors with this card. Keep code examples intact.
